title: cymbopogon collinus
intro: Cymbopogon collinus, also known as the Indian grass or Indian thyme grass, is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Cymbopogon collinus include its potential to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and show antiseptic properties, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Cymbopogon collinus can be found in the form of essential oils, infusions, and decoctions, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
